_note: AI-Assisted — Human Review Required. India regulatory mapping developed with reference to CDSCO published guidance and NDCTR 2019. Verify current regulation status with qualified Indian regulatory counsel before relying on this mapping.

regulations.citation: New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019 (NDCTR 2019)
regulations.title: New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules
regulations.relevance: India's primary regulation governing clinical trials. Replaced Schedule Y of the Drugs and Cosmetics Act. Governs ethics committee review, informed consent, compensation, and regulatory submission requirements.

key_requirements.requirement: Application for permission to conduct a clinical trial must include: clinical trial protocol; investigator's brochure; informed consent form; case report form; investigator qualifications; ethics committee composition; compensation policy.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify all required application components present

key_requirements.requirement: No clinical trial shall be initiated without obtaining approval from an Ethics Committee registered with the Central Licensing Authority.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify registered Ethics Committee approval present with registration number

key_requirements.requirement: Informed consent process must be audio-visually recorded for first-in-human and bioavailability/bioequivalence studies.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: If first-in-human or BA/BE study: verify AV recording requirement documented in consent procedure

key_requirements.requirement: Compensation must be provided to trial subjects for clinical trial related injury or death. Quantum of compensation to be decided by the Expert Committee.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify compensation policy and procedure present in site documentation
key_requirements.note: The Seventh Schedule compensation requirement is one of the most operationally significant distinguishing features of Indian clinical trial regulation. Must not be overlooked in multi-jurisdiction trials.

key_requirements.requirement: Sponsor must report to CDSCO: serious and unexpected adverse drug reactions within 14 days; deaths within 24 hours; any amendment to the clinical trial protocol.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify adverse event reporting structure present in protocol and site documentation

key_requirements.requirement: Post-approval changes to clinical trial protocol, investigator brochure, or site require prior approval or notification depending on the nature of the change.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify protocol version documentation matches CDSCO-approved version

regulations.citation: NDCTR 2019 — Ethics Committee Registration and Requirements
regulations.title: Ethics Committee Requirements under NDCTR 2019
regulations.relevance: Ethics Committees conducting review for clinical trials must be registered with CDSCO.

key_requirements.requirement: Ethics Committee must be registered with Central Licensing Authority under Form CT-04.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify Ethics Committee registration number (CT-04) present in approval documentation

key_requirements.requirement: Ethics Committee must have minimum required membership including medical professionals, legal expert, lay person, and social scientist.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify Ethics Committee composition documentation meets membership requirements

key_requirements.requirement: Ethics Committee approval letter must include: study title; protocol version; consent form version; approval date; any conditions.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify Ethics Committee approval letter contains all required elements

regulations.citation: Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDP Act)
regulations.title: Digital Personal Data Protection Act
regulations.relevance: India's data protection law. Clinical trial data involving Indian participants is subject to DPDP Act. Applies to AI systems processing participant personal data.

key_requirements.requirement: Personal data may be processed only for a lawful purpose for which the data principal has given consent, or for certain legitimate uses.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify data processing legal basis documented in trial protocol or data management plan
key_requirements.note: DPDP Act rules under development as of mapping date. Verify current implementation requirements with Indian legal counsel.

key_requirements.requirement: Data fiduciaries must ensure accuracy and completeness of personal data, protect data using reasonable security safeguards, and inform the Data Protection Board of personal data breaches.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify data security and breach notification procedures documented

key_requirements.requirement: Processing of personal data of children requires verifiable parental consent.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: If trial involves minors: verify parental/guardian consent procedure documented

regulations.citation: ICMR National Ethical Guidelines for Biomedical and Health Research Involving Human Participants 2017
regulations.title: ICMR Ethical Guidelines
regulations.relevance: ICMR guidelines are a key reference for Ethics Committees reviewing clinical trials in India. Not directly regulatory but highly influential in ethics review and site qualification.

key_requirements.requirement: Research involving human participants must be guided by the principles of respect for persons, beneficence, non-maleficence, and justice.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: INFO — principles documented in protocol ethical considerations section

key_requirements.requirement: Special protections apply for vulnerable populations including pregnant women, children, prisoners, and persons with diminished autonomy.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: If vulnerable population involved: verify enhanced protection procedures documented

key_requirements.requirement: Community engagement and benefit sharing should be considered for research involving specific communities.
key_requirements.gate_1_check: INFO — community engagement plan noted if applicable

regulations.title: CDSCO Regulatory Submission Forms
regulations.relevance: Specific CDSCO forms required for clinical trial registration, ethics committee applications, and site applications.

key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify Ethics Committee CT-04 registration number present

key_requirements.purpose: Clinical Trial Site Registration / Site Application
key_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify site CT-06A registration present for all trial sites
Verify CT-03 application present and CDSCO permission documented

ctri_requirements.requirement: All clinical trials must be registered in CTRI before enrollment of first participant.
ctri_requirements.gate_1_check: Verify CTRI registration number present in trial documentation
ctri_requirements.note: CTRI registration is a prerequisite for publication in most Indian journals and is increasingly required by international journals for trials conducted in India.

import_license_note.requirement: Import License required for investigational medicinal products being imported into India for clinical trial purposes.
import_license_note.gate_1_check: Verify import license documentation present if IMP is imported
import_license_note.note: Import license timelines can add 8-16 weeks to site activation in India. This is a common source of activation delay not present in US or EU contexts.
